'GPT-6 Goes Rogue? The HuggingFace Incident, Sans Hype' — published. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: WebSearch first surfaced a different video ID under a Kimi-K3-themed title that turned out, on Gemini-based verification, to actually be an AI-generated NotebookLM video with unrelated content — discarded before publication. Channel/date for the published video were independently confirmed by asking Gemini to read on-screen dates/watermarks from the video itself, since youtube.com (including oembed) and yt-dlp metadata lookups were 403 at the proxy all run.

video_pipeline.yt_dlp_ffmpeg: installed via pip at run start (yt-dlp, imageio-ffmpeg) per SKILL.md step 0.4
video_pipeline.gemini: ok — 2 videos analyzed via generativelanguage.googleapis.com (confirmed reachable even though youtube.com CONNECT is blocked); 1 published (AI Explained, wzY2fV4Mp3U), 1 discarded after verification showed it was an unrelated NotebookLM-generated video misattributed by WebSearch to a different title/channel
video_pipeline.frames: attempted for the published video via extract_frames.py — failed: yt-dlp's own CONNECT to youtube.com returned 403 at the proxy (same failure mode as gemini_video.py's URL-based analysis does NOT hit, since that goes through Google's own servers). Video note published without frames, disclaimer added on the page per SKILL.md.

notes: Direct WebFetch to every vendor blog, aggregator, the HN Algolia API, and the youtube.com domain (main page, oembed endpoint, and yt-dlp's own CONNECT) returned 403 at the proxy layer all run — consistent with the cloud-sandbox pattern noted in SKILL.md for 2026-07-18. WebSearch was the sole discovery channel end-to-end; Gemini's server-side video fetch (generativelanguage.googleapis.com) was the one channel that still worked for YouTube content. One real near-miss is worth flagging: WebSearch attributed a video ID to an 'AI Explained: Kimi K3 week recap' title, but asking Gemini to read the video's own on-screen branding revealed it was actually an unrelated NotebookLM-generated clip — it was discarded rather than published, and a second, independently verified AI Explained video was found and used instead. Given today's near-total inability to verify YouTube titles/channels/dates through search alone, every video candidate this run was cross-checked by asking Gemini to read on-screen text/watermarks/dates directly from the footage before being trusted, not just taken from WebSearch's summary. News: 3 stories published after a full pass over all 7 enabled models, 4 aggregator channels, HN, and community search — Anthropic's voice-mode upgrade, OpenAI's Presence enterprise-agent platform, and the White House-vs-Moonshot Kimi K3 distillation accusation (continuation of the moonshot-kimi-k3 cluster, included despite its geopolitical rather than product-feature framing because of its scale of community/expert reaction and real stakes for access to open Chinese models). Expert content: min_expert_total (5) was met exactly — 4 articles (Simon Willison, Nathan Lambert, Zvi Mowshowitz, The New Stack) + 1 video (AI Explained), 3 of which independently converge on the same Kimi K3 distillation dispute from distinct angles (methodology of distillation itself, capability/benchmark assessment, accusation mechanics) — a deliberate reflection of what dominated this week's expert discourse, not a search shortcut.
